problem: Aurora audio is the current weakest quality category; the lab keeps cue-family candidate history tied to the latest measured event-gap risks.
strategy: Group cue families by player meaning, attach current event-gap risk, attach focused candidate-loop history, and allow runtime promotion only after measured keeper gates plus full audio/semantic/quality checks.
successMeasure: Move audio toward 7.5+/10 while preserving 9/9 cue alignment, semantic audio near 9.78/10, and overall quality >= 9.2/10.
promotionRule: This analyzer records keeper candidates but does not edit runtime cue definitions. Runtime promotion requires a keeper, a refreshed full theme comparison, no cue-alignment regression, no semantic regression, and overall quality >= 9.2.

summary.nextGap: Tune the highest segment-level gap next: challengePerfect onset. Rerun audio comparison and event-gap analysis after the change.

families.label: Formation / Stage Ambience
families.problem: Make the board cadence create Galaga-like pressure without crowding combat cues.
families.playerMeaning: Players should feel the alien rack is alive and advancing even before an immediate threat fires.

cues.playerMeaning: Compare ongoing formation cadence and whether gameplay feels like it has enough marching pressure.

currentRisk.recommendation: Keep as secondary pass after higher-risk audio gaps are narrowed.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

families.label: Impact / Explosion Feedback
families.problem: Separate hit confirmation, boss damage, normal destruction, and boss destruction so combat outcomes are legible.
families.playerMeaning: Players should immediately understand hit, damage, destruction, and boss hierarchy from sound alone.

cues.playerMeaning: A hit connected; the player should hear immediate damage feedback before destruction hierarchy.

currentRisk.recommendation: Prioritize impact/explosion event clarity so the player understands damage, kill, and boss multi-hit state.

referenceWindow.selection: later Zako phrase reserved for immediate hit confirmation, separated from the normal-enemy destruction subwindow

referenceSegmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 0.75-0.99s Zako window; enemy hit is scored as immediate damage confirmation

families.label: Reward / Loss Feedback
families.problem: Make ship loss, rescue reward, and perfect challenge events emotionally clear and arcade-readable.
families.playerMeaning: Players should hear regret, reward, and celebration as distinct state changes.

cues.playerMeaning: The player lost a ship; the cue should be unmistakable and synchronized with death feedback.

currentRisk.recommendation: Keep playerHit as a calibrated guardrail for now: raw body/tail risk matches synthetic Galaga browser-capture behavior, so the next runtime work should target a non-calibrated cue gap.

referenceSegmentation.source: curated from active-window reads in aurora-audio-theme-comparison and repeated playerHit candidate pass
referenceSegmentation.resolution: relative to full galaga3-death clip; labels preserve long death onset, sustained body, and final tail

decision.reason: Challenge Perfect candidate clears measured calibrated role/segment keeper gates.

runtimeTrial.reason: Runtime Challenge Perfect candidates remain rejected after confirmation: focused keepers and prechecks can look promising, but repeated live full-theme captures are volatile; runtime was reverted to the stable reference phrase while activity-span and calibrated segment gates remain in the harness.
runtimeTrial.nextStep: Do not promote Challenge Perfect runtime audio yet. Keep the new harness evidence, stabilize repeated full-theme capture, then compare bossHit, challengeTransition, and gameOver for the next best audio return.

runtimeTrial.reason: Challenge Perfect runtime trial rejected: the focused/stability-gated candidate improved the average worst-segment rollup, but live full-theme recapture worsened Challenge Perfect itself to 7.7/10 as the highest audio risk, so this is preserved as generator evidence and not promoted.
runtimeTrial.nextStep: Do not promote Challenge Perfect from isolated onset/body candidates. Replace the next audio strategy with full-phrase/segment-boundary work: stabilize the scorer on canonical reference-vs-reference capture, then generate candidates that optimize onset, body, tail, and live capture segmentation together.

decision.reason: Challenge Perfect runtime trial rejected: the focused/stability-gated candidate improved the average worst-segment rollup, but live full-theme recapture worsened Challenge Perfect itself to 7.7/10 as the highest audio risk, so this is preserved as generator evidence and not promoted.

decision.recommendation: challengePerfect: Do not promote Challenge Perfect from isolated onset/body candidates.
